text_blocks.text: Muhammad Ali Pasha evolved the military from
one that convened under the tradition of the corvee to a
great modernised army. He introduced conscription of the
male peasantry in 19th century Egypt, and took a novel
approach to create his great army, strengthening it with
numbers and in skill. Education and training of the new
soldiers was not an option; the new concepts were
furthermore enforced by isolation. The men were held in
barracks to avoid distraction of their growth as a
military unit to be reckoned with. The resentment for the
military way of life eventually faded from the men and
a new ideology took hold, one of nationalism and pride.
It was with the help of this newly reborn martial unit
that Muhammad Ali imposed his rule over Egypt.
